name: Thailand
overview: Thailand's Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (2019, 'PDPA') is a GDPR-modelled comprehensive law fully in force since June 2022 after pandemic-era deferrals. The Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC) has issued a steady stream of sub-regulations on security measures, breach notification, DPOs and cross-border transfers, and began issuing administrative fines in 2024.

legal_instruments.title: Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (2019)
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law with GDPR-style lawful bases, sensitive-data rules, data subject rights, controller/processor duties, DPO requirements, 72-hour breach notification and administrative fines up to THB 5 million.

legal_instruments.title: PDPC Notification on Criteria for Cross-Border Transfer of Personal Data (2023)
legal_instruments.summary: Sub-regulations under ss.28-29 PDPA establishing adequacy criteria, binding corporate rules and standard contractual clause mechanisms (ASEAN MCC and EU SCC-compatible) for overseas transfers.

legal_instruments.title: PDPC Notification on Personal Data Breach Notification (2022)
legal_instruments.summary: Prescribes 72-hour notification to the PDPC of breaches with risk to rights and freedoms, and communication to data subjects where high risk.

developments.title: First major PDPA administrative fine issued
developments.summary: The PDPC expert committee fined a large e-commerce operator THB 7 million (across violations) for failing to appoint a DPO, inadequate security and failure to notify a breach — Thailand's first headline enforcement.

developments.title: PDPC steps up proactive inspections
developments.summary: The PDPC announced expanded audit and inspection programs targeting data brokers and organisations with repeated breach reports.

penalties: Administrative fines up to THB 5 million per violation; criminal penalties (up to 1 year imprisonment / THB 1 million) for unlawful disclosure of sensitive data; punitive damages up to 2x actual damages in civil claims.
cross_border: Transfers require adequate destination protection or safeguards (BCRs, standard contractual clauses, certification) or a statutory exception (consent, contract necessity, etc.) under ss.28-29 and 2023 PDPC notifications.
